event: Studio Museum founded by Eleanor Holmes Norton, Carter Burden, Charles E. Inniss, Campbell Wylly, Betty Blayton-Taylor, Frank Donnelly
Inaugural exhibition: Tom Lloyd, Electronic Refractions II (24 Sept)
Studio Program proposed by William T. Williams
Studio Program / Artist-in-Residence Program launched
Charles E. Inniss departs; Edward S. Spriggs becomes director
Black Masters series begins
Spriggs departs; Courtney Callender becomes director
Fine Art of Collecting seminar launches
Callender departs; Mary Schmidt Campbell becomes director
New York Bank of Savings donates 144 W 125th St building to museum
James Van Der Zee Archive acquired
Groundbreaking on 144 W 125th renovation (J. Max Bond Jr. architect)
Reopens at 144 W 125th with Ritual and Myth: A Survey of African American Art
Tradition and Conflict exhibition (150 works / 55 artists)
Hale Woodruff Memorial Exhibition series debuts
Contemporary African Artists: Changing Tradition exhibits at Venice Biennale
The Decade Show collaboration opens
Sculpture Garden opens with The Listening Sky
Mary Schmidt Campbell departs (museum-recorded end of directorship 2000); Lowery Stokes Sims becomes director
Expanding the Walls high-school photography program launches
Uptown Fridays begins
Freestyle exhibition (curated by Thelma Golden); first F-show; introduces 'post-Black' framing
Harlem Postcards project launches
StudioSound music commissioning series begins
Sims departs; Thelma Golden becomes director and chief curator
Studio magazine launched
Frequency exhibition (second F-show; 35 emerging Black artists)
Joyce Alexander Wein Artist Prize established (George Wein endowment)
Flow exhibition (third F-show)
Target Free Sundays launched
The Bearden Project (100 artists responding to Romare Bearden); Studio Lab launched
Fore exhibition (fourth F-show; Naima Keith / Lauren Haynes / Thomas J. Lax)
Joint MoMA-Studio Museum curatorial fellowship established
Studio magazine wins AAM Museum Publications Design Competition first prize
Adjaye Associates commissioned to design new building
inHarlem initiative announces outdoor sculpture installations
Fictions exhibition (fifth F-show; 19 emerging artists of African descent)
Peggy Cooper Cafritz bequest delivers 400+ contemporary artworks
Closure of 144 W 125th building for demolition and rebuild
Capital campaign (Creating Space) reported above $175M target
Groundbreaking on Adjaye-designed new building
MoMA PS1 partnership hosts AIR culminating exhibitions during construction
James Van Der Zee Archive partnership with Metropolitan Museum announced
MacKenzie Scott endowment gift announced
Capital campaign reaches $210M (per The Art Newspaper)
Mellon $10M Capital Campaign grant

Ford Foundation $10M endowment grant for Director and Chief Curator position; on Thelma Golden's 20th anniversary
New 82000 sq ft Adjaye-designed building opens to the public; Tom Lloyd retrospective inaugural
2026 Artist-in-Residence cohort (Pharr / Quamina / Simmons) is first to occupy J. Bruce Llewellyn AIR Center
